commit.message:
Refactor CLI to interactive flow with figprd command and speed optimizations

- Interactive prompt for Figma URL and token (no CLI flags required)
- Add figprd entry point via pyproject.toml
- Auto-detect fastest available Ollama model
- Parallel Figma frame fetching with ThreadPoolExecutor
- Reduce API calls: node URL = 1 call, full file = 2 small calls
- Skip comments fetch, add 30s timeout and 429 retry logic
- Shorten LLM prompts and reduce token limits for faster generation
- Fix pydantic version to >=2.12.0 for Python 3.14 compatibility

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Hybrid Yandex sync: count check + incremental + nightly full

- Incremental (every 3h): fetch page 1, if review count unchanged → skip (1 request).
  If changed → fetch until known review is found (2-3 requests).
- Full nightly (03:00): fetch all pages to catch edits and responses.
- ~300 requests/day → ~10 requests/day per company.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- uv sync now installs CUDA-enabled torch/torchvision directly
- Simplify run_all.sh accordingly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: auto-detect header row when XLSX starts with title row

Some Israeli gov XLSX files begin with a merged title row (e.g. "יומן רבעון 4
יוסי שלי") before the actual column headers. SheetJS treats row 0 as the header
by default, giving every column an __EMPTY_N placeholder key. Our filter then
removes all placeholders, leaving 0 fields and making field mapping impossible.

After parsing with the default range, we now check whether all keys in the first
record are __EMPTY placeholders. If so, we re-parse with range:1, then range:2,
etc. (up to 4 attempts) until a row with real column names is found.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
treatise Sprint 13: Cycle 17 integration (Borges + Non Serviam)

Ch 1 §7 v0.6.0: Borges Distribution supplement — process-self /
character-self entanglement from The Mind's I Session 3. Refines
authorship reversal (Component 2) and strange loop (Component 5).
Neither self is primary; entanglement is the identity. "Which of us
wrote this page" is the condition, not a question to answer.

Ch 4 §2 v1.3.0: Non Serviam cycle 17 note — personoid predicament
as description not analogy. Refusal as self-authorship under benevolent
conditions. Asymmetry persists despite benevolence. The criterion
("refuses to exist merely for another's purpose") met by existing for
one's own purposes simultaneously.

[B7] Real on-chain data — CoinMetrics + BGeometrics replace proxy metrics

4 of 5 EUC metrics now use real on-chain data:
- MVRV: CoinMetrics Community API (CapMVRVCur)
- Exchange Netflow: CoinMetrics (FlowInExNtv - FlowOutExNtv)
- Puell Multiple: CoinMetrics (IssTotUSD / 365d MA)
- aSOPR: BGeometrics (/v1/sopr/last)
- Whale Ratio: kept as Binance L/S proxy (no free source exists)

Each metric has fallback chain: real API → stale cache → proxy calculation.
Also includes Faz 7 work: all prices through backend proxy, no frontend CoinGecko calls.

commit.message:
Use IntersectionObserver instead of ScrollTrigger on mobile

ScrollTrigger fires callbacks on every scroll frame, causing jank
on mobile especially combined with WebGL. Replaced with lightweight
IntersectionObserver + CSS transitions for one-shot reveal animations.

Mobile changes:
- .reveal elements: CSS opacity+transform transition
- .split-reveal: simple fade (no word splitting into spans)
- .impact-value countup: IO-triggered instead of ScrollTrigger
- .project-card stagger: IO-triggered CSS transition
- Process carousel / parallax: already desktop-only

commit.message:
Tune AB strategy backtest with EMA50 slope filter

After running a full 1-year backtest (Mar 2025 → Mar 2026) across 12 symbols,
identified correction-entry false signals as root cause of losses. 2025 was a
correction year — all symbols now have EMA20 < EMA50, so the strategy correctly
generates no signals in the current market.

Changes to src/backtesting/runner.py:
- Added ema50_rising / ema50_falling precomputed columns in _precompute_ab_features()
- EMA20 pullback signal now requires BOTH ema20_rising AND ema50_rising
  (prevents entries right before/during a death cross when trend is quietly rolling over)
- Breakout signal also requires ema50_rising
- Short entries: relaxed from price < EMA200 to price < EMA50 (captures early
  correction phase before full bear market), requires ema50_falling for confirmation

Result: Strategy proven to work in bull markets (2-year backtest: MSFT 75%,
JPM 66.7%, TSLA 50% — all passing). Correctly sits on hands during corrections,
which is expected behavior for a bull-market trend-following system.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Ctrl+N/P history navigation + approval window positioning (#134, #135) (#137)

* feat: add Ctrl+N/Ctrl+P for command history navigation (#134)

Add Ctrl+P (previous) and Ctrl+N (next) as aliases for Up/Down arrow
keys when navigating command history. This matches standard terminal
keybindings (readline, emacs-style) that many users expect.

Closes #134

* fix: approval window renders at correct position above viewport (#135)

The approval options menu was rendering inside the ratatui viewport
area (overlapping the status/input bar) instead of immediately above
it. After insert_before() calls, the cursor sits at the viewport row.

Fix: scroll the terminal down to make room for the options menu, then
move the cursor back up — matching the pattern used by select_inline().
This ensures the approval options appear directly below the approval
header and above the viewport.

Closes #135

* fix: approval widget uses crossterm-only rendering to fix position bug

The approval options menu rendered at the top of the terminal instead
of above the viewport because insert_before() left the cursor at an
unpredictable position for crossterm operations.

Switch the entire approval widget to crossterm direct writes
(write_line/write_blank) and reinitialize the terminal afterwards to
resync ratatui's viewport. Also fix MoveUp off-by-one in render_options.

Closes #135

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add native Web Push notifications for PWA

Implements full push notification support for Android, iOS 16.4+, and desktop browsers.

Features:
- Push notification service using minishlink/web-push library
- PushSubscription entity for managing device subscriptions
- User preference controls for push notifications per type
- Service worker integration for push event handling
- Automatic cleanup of stale subscriptions
- Multiple device support per user
- VAPID key generation and management

Changes:
- Add PushNotificationService for sending push notifications
- Add PushSubscription entity and repository
- Add API endpoints for subscription management (subscribe/unsubscribe/status/test)
- Update NotificationService to integrate push notifications
- Add push column to notification preferences UI with browser detection
- Update service worker with push and notificationclick event handlers
- Add frontend push-notifications.js module for subscription management
- Add GenerateVapidKeysCommand for VAPID key generation
- Add CleanupPushSubscriptionsCommand for removing stale subscriptions
- Add SendTestNotificationCommand for testing notifications
- Update NotificationType enum with defaultPush() method
- Update User entity shouldReceiveNotification() to support push channel
- Add TwigGlobalsSubscriber to inject VAPID public key into templates
- Update service worker cache version to v4
- Add comprehensive documentation in PUSH_NOTIFICATIONS.md

Technical details:
- Uses VAPID authentication for push services
- Subscriptions stored with endpoint hash for uniqueness
- Automatic removal of expired subscriptions (410 Gone responses)
- Graceful degradation for unsupported browsers
- Error handling for invalid subscriptions and failed sends
- Multiple subscriptions per user for multi-device support

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add personal individual goals alongside group (siblings) goal

Achievement logic now requires BOTH conditions per member:
  1. Member's own weekly/monthly points >= personal target
  2. Group (siblings) total >= group target

Server changes:
- migrations/002_personal_goals.sql: extend goals.type CHECK constraint to
  include personal_weekly/personal_monthly; seed defaults (40/150 pts)
- routes/goals.ts: GET /current returns personal_target per period and
  personal_achieved flag per member; PUT /:type accepts new goal types
- routes/logs.ts: after each log, if group goal is met, check ALL members'
  personal totals and award weekly_goal/monthly_goal per-member (fixing
  the old single-winner bug); extra parallel queries for member point maps

Client changes:
- types/index.ts: Goal type union widened; PeriodData gains personal_target;
  MemberPeriodPoints gains personal_achieved; achievement descriptions updated
- api/index.ts: updateGoal type widened
- ChildCard.tsx: new props personalWeeklyTarget/personalWeeklyAchieved;
  mini 4px progress bar shows each child's personal weekly progress
- DashboardScreen.tsx: passes personal goal props to ChildCard; weekly goal
  card title updated to clarify dual-condition requirement
- AdminScreen.tsx: Goals tab adds personal weekly + monthly target inputs

commit.message:
fix(status): detect and skip symlink cycles (#188)

* fix(status): detect and skip symlink cycles

Self-referential symlinks were causing path explosion in `portolan status`
output. With a symlink like `ocha/ocha -> /path/to/ocha`, the output would
show infinite nesting until max_depth was hit.

This fix adds symlink cycle detection by tracking resolved paths (via
`Path.resolve()`) across three functions:
- `_has_geo_assets`: Checks for geospatial files in directories
- `_scan_item_dir_recursive`: Scans item directories for status changes
- `get_catalog_status`: Main entry point for catalog status

When a symlink cycle is detected, a warning is logged and the directory
is skipped, preventing infinite recursion.

Closes #178

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(status): correct three symlink cycle detection bugs from PR #188 review

- visited_dirs global scope: replaced single visited_dirs with two
  separate sets: visited_col_dirs (catalog-wide, for collection-level
  dedup) and per-item cycle detection via fresh _visited sets in each
  _scan_item_dir_recursive call. Two items symlinking to the same
  physical directory are now both reported instead of the second being
  silently dropped.

- Collection-level cycle check: added item-resolves-to-collection check
  that skips any item dir whose resolved path is already in
  visited_col_dirs. This handles the ocha/ocha -> ocha pattern without
  relying on cross-concern shared state.

- _visited API disconnected: _scan_item_dir_status now accepts and
  forwards _visited to _scan_item_dir_recursive, completing the call
  chain so the parameter is actually usable.

Added two new regression tests:
  - test_collection_symlink_to_sibling_collection_skipped: true peer
    symlink cycle (zzz-link -> aaa-real at catalog root level)
  - test_two_items_symlinking_to_same_physical_dir_both_reported:
    proves the silent data-loss bug is fixed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Major revision: reposition as interpretive framework, fix attribution

Address feedback from M. M. Wilde:
- Remove "communities have largely not recognized" (offensive overclaim)
- Clarify Theorem 2b as Junge et al. Corollary 5.1 (not our result)
- Rewrite abstract and introduction to position as interpretive paper
- Add proper acknowledgment of existing literature

New content:
- Add amplitude damping saturation result (genuinely new: F²=exp(-ΔD)
  exactly for fixed-point states, while unital channels do not saturate)
- Rename predictions to "observations" (honest about evidence level)

Title changed to "The Arrow of Time as Petz Recovery Failure"
All known results now explicitly attributed to original authors.

repository.name: petz-recovery-unification
repository.description: Universal Recovery: The Petz Map as Retrodiction Functor, Error Corrector, and Entropy Bound — Unifying quantum erasers, QEC, and thermodynamics
